decks.notes: The main concept of this Dragon Stompy deck is to quickly ramp up mana using City of Traitors, Ancient Tomb, and Chrome Mox to play powerful threats such as Broadside Bombardiers and Hexing Squelchers. The deck also focuses on disrupting opponents' strategies with cards like Chalice of the Void and Sundering Eruption. The inclusion of cards like The One Ring and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker allows for additional synergy and card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with aggressive plays and efficient resource management.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with small but efficient creatures, taking advantage of cards like Squadron Hawk and Windbrisk Heights to maintain card advantage and board presence. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Esper Sentinel disrupt the opponent's game plan, while Static Prison and Wasteland help control the board. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Chrome Mox provide additional support and versatility. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain tempo to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on using a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It utilizes powerful control elements like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Brainstorm to maintain board control and answer threats. Boseiju, Who Endures provides additional protection for key spells, while Leyline Binding and Quantum Riddler offer disruption and card advantage. The addition of powerful threats like Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath and Murktide Regent ensures the deck can close out the game once it has taken control.

The key concept of the UR Aggro Legacy deck is to maintain board control and aggressive pressure through a combination of efficient burn spells and disruptive tempo plays. The deck focuses on early game aggression with cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent, while also utilizing counterspells like Daze and Force of Will to protect its threats. The deck's ability to pivot between aggression and disruption allows it to adapt to different matchups and assert dominance in the early game. By utilizing card advantage engines like Brainstorm and Mishra's Bauble, the deck can maintain consistency and outmaneuver opponents.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying pressure through aggressive creatures. It utilizes cards like Swords to Plowshares, Thougthseize, and Skyclave Apparition to disrupt the opponent, while deploying threats like Overlord of the Balemurk and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to quickly close out the game. The deck also has a toolbox aspect, with Recruiter of the Guard fetching key creatures like Stoneforge Mystic. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad allows for additional value and synergy with the deck's flicker effects. Overall, the deck aims to control the game while applying constant pressure to the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping into powerful threats such as Magus of the Moon and Broadside Bombardiers while disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Chalice of the Void and Blood Moon. The goal is to lock down the opponent's resources and apply pressure with aggressive creatures, all while using fast mana acceleration like Simian Spirit Guide and Chrome Mox. The deck also includes powerful removal spells like Sundering Eruption to deal with any problematic threats the opponent may play.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the graveyard as a resource to quickly generate tokens, set up combos, and overwhelm opponents with a powerful board presence. By utilizing cards like Faithless Looting, Golgari Grave-Troll, and Cabal Therapy, along with recursion effects like Bridge from Below and Dread Return, the deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with creatures and then bring them back onto the battlefield quickly and repeatedly. With cards like Narcomoeba, Stinkweed Imp, and Poxwalkers, the deck can swarm the board and create overwhelming pressure on opponents.

This control deck focuses on utilizing lands as a resource to disrupt the opponent's game plan and ultimately win the game. By using cards like Exploration and Life from the Loam to ramp and recur lands, along with cards like Wasteland and Ghost Quarter to disrupt the opponent's mana base, the deck aims to control the game tempo. Additionally, the deck utilizes combo pieces like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage to quickly assemble a powerful threat. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent through land-based control strategies.

This deck focuses on aggressive tactics with a mix of disruption, control, and tempo plays. The key strategy is to quickly deploy threats like Orcish Bowmasters and Barrowgoyf while using counter spells and removal like Force of Will, Fatal Push, and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Cards like Brazen Borrower and Murktide Regent provide additional flexibility and board presence. The deck aims to maintain a fast pace and keep the opponent on the back foot, ultimately overwhelming them with a combination of aggression and control.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize Cloudpost and ramping cards such as Grim Monolith to quickly generate a large amount of mana. This allows for the early deployment of powerful Eldrazi creatures like Ulamog, Emrakul, and Kozilek, as well as Planeswalkers like Karn and Ugin. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to control the opponent's spells. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with giant threats and powerful abilities while using the ramp engine to get ahead in mana.

This deck focuses on controlling the board while applying pressure through aggressive creatures in order to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain tempo. Key cards like Murktide Regent and Thoughtseize help to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Daze provide additional control options. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to deal with threats and keep the opponent on the back foot. Utilizing a mix of disruption, aggression, and control, this deck aims to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory.

The main concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to aggressively play powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer. By utilizing mana acceleration from Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal, this deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with hard-hitting threats like Reality Smasher and It That Heralds the End. The inclusion of cards like Wastes and Wastescape+Battlemage','','');">Wastescape Battlemage provide additional flexibility and disruption, making this deck a formidable force in the Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

The focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also developing its own board presence with powerful threats like Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath and Murktide Regent. By efficiently managing resources and utilizing versatile cards like Prismatic Ending and Force of Will, the deck seeks to outmaneuver opponents and maintain control of the game until it can secure a win condition.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, then using cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back into play for a low cost. Utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with high-powered threats early in the game. The key strategy is to efficiently reanimate big threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan through hand disruption and counter spells.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with efficient creatures. The deck utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's hand and spells, while also using cards like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares to close out the game quickly. The deck also includes a variety of control elements like Fatal Push and Daze to keep the opponent on their toes. The goal is to maintain board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy to secure a victory.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize efficient creature threats paired with disruptive spells and card draw to control the tempo of the game. By playing aggressively with cards like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, while also keeping the opponent on their toes with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, the deck aims to maintain an advantage on the board and in hand. Utilizing the deck's mana base of dual lands and fetch lands, along with utility lands like Wasteland, helps ensure consistent and smooth gameplay for this aggressive tempo strategy.
